name: Sorare Fantasy Football
description: GraphQL API for Sorare's NFT-based fantasy football, baseball, and basketball games. Query cards, players, lineups, transfer market, and your portfolio with natural-language commands. Handles Sorare's bcrypt-salted login and 30-day JWT caching automatically.
instructions:
## Authentication (handled automatically)

Sorare uses a custom two-step login the AnythingMCP `LOGIN_TOKEN` engine performs for you:

1. `GET https://api.sorare.com/api/v1/users/{email}` returns the account's bcrypt salt.
2. Your plain password is hashed locally with `bcrypt.hashSync(password, salt)`.
3. The hash is sent through Sorare's `signIn` GraphQL mutation; Sorare returns a JWT valid for ~30 days, tagged with the audience claim `anythingmcp` (managed for you).
4. Every subsequent call adds `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` and `JWT-AUD: anythingmcp` headers.

The token is cached in-memory and in the encrypted `connector_auth_cache` table, then proactively re-issued ~24 h before expiry and on any 401. You never need to refresh manually.

## Credentials

- `SORARE_EMAIL` — your Sorare account email.
- `SORARE_PASSWORD` — your plain account password (never stored or transmitted in plain text after the first login; only the bcrypt hash leaves your server).

## 2FA

If your account has 2FA enabled, the `signIn` mutation will fail until you provide a fresh `otpAttempt`. For headless/server use we recommend creating a dedicated read-only Sorare account without 2FA. Set `SORARE_PASSWORD` to that account's password.

## Money & pricing conventions — READ THIS FIRST

Sorare exposes amounts in **four parallel scales** on every `MonetaryAmount`:
- `wei` → raw on-chain unit (1 ETH = 10^18 wei). String to avoid precision loss.
- `eurCents`, `usdCents`, `gbpCents` → **integers in cents, divide by 100 for the currency unit.** Example: `eurCents: 354` means **€3.54**, not €354.
- These can be `null` when the card is listed only in crypto (no fiat conversion exposed). When you see `eurCents: null`, fall back to `wei` (roughly: `wei * 1e-18 * <ETH/EUR>` for an EUR estimate; the live ETH/EUR rate is not provided by the API).

### TokenOffer sides — easy to get wrong

For a **single-sale offer** (someone selling a card):
- `senderSide.anyCards` = the cards being sold (sender = seller, sending the card).
- `receiverSide.amounts` = the price the buyer pays (receiver = buyer, receiving the card).

If you query `senderSide.amounts` on a sale offer you'll get **zeros** because the seller isn't sending money. Always read prices from `receiverSide.amounts`.

## When dedicated tools are not enough

The purpose-built tools below cover the most common workflows. For anything else use the generic helpers:
- `sorare_graphql_schema` (no args) → compact summary of Query/Mutation + a flat index of every type.
- `sorare_graphql_schema(type: "TypeName")` → just one type's definition.
- `sorare_graphql_schema(search: "keyword")` → every type whose name or a field name matches.
- `sorare_graphql_query` / `_mutation` / `_subscription` → execute arbitrary documents.

**GraphQL introspection (`__schema` / `__type`) is disabled on Sorare's production endpoint.** Use `sorare_graphql_schema(type: "…")` instead.

## Useful field paths the schema search won't surface obviously

- `Player.lowestPriceAnyCard(rarity, inSeason)` → floor card for a player at a rarity. (Wrapped by `sorare_player_floor_price`.)
- `Player.so5Scores(last, position?)` → recent So5 scores for form analysis. (Wrapped by `sorare_player_recent_scores`.)
- `tokens.tokenPrices(playerSlug, rarity, first, season?)` → recent sale history for a player+rarity, in fiat + wei. (Wrapped by `sorare_token_prices`.)
- `tokens.liveSingleSaleOffers(playerSlug?, sport?, first)` → current secondary market. (Wrapped by `sorare_live_sale_offers`.)
- `currentUser.cards(first, rarities, sport)` → your inventory. NOT `currentUser.football.myCards` (that path doesn't exist).
- `currentUser.availableBalances` → wallet in wei + EUR + USD + GBP. (Wrapped by `sorare_wallet_balance`.)
- For free-text player search use the root `searchPlayers(query, pageSize)` then drill via `commonPlayerHits { anyPlayer { ... on Player { ... } } }`. There is no `football.players(search:…)` — that path doesn't exist.

## Sport / season / rarity enum quirks

- `Sport` is upper-snake: `FOOTBALL`, `BASEBALL`, `NBA`.
- `Rarity` is lowercase: `common`, `limited`, `rare`, `super_rare`, `unique`, `custom_series` (not the upper-snake form).
- `season` arguments are season **start years**: 2024 means the 2024-25 season.
- Older-vintage cards (pre-current-season) still score normally in So5 Classic competitions; the vintage mainly matters for the Captain Bonus and for season-eligibility tournaments.

## Rate limits

Sorare enforces per-IP and per-token rate limits (`429`). Heavy bulk-export workflows should batch and back-off. Very large GraphQL responses (hundreds of items × deep selection) may also time out — split the request.

## Useful starter prompts

- "What's my Sorare wallet balance?" → `sorare_wallet_balance`
- "What's the floor price for a Limited Calafiori right now?" → `sorare_player_floor_price(playerSlug, rarity:"limited")`
- "What did Messi rare cards sell for in the last month?" → `sorare_token_prices(playerSlug, rarity:"rare", first:30)`
- "Show me my Limited cards for Top-5-league players." → `sorare_list_my_cards(rarities:["limited"])`
- "Is this player in form?" → `sorare_player_recent_scores(playerSlug, last:10)`
- "How am I doing in So5 overall?" → `sorare_my_trophies_summary(sport:"FOOTBALL")`

## Raw-query gotchas (read before writing a `sorare_graphql_query`)

Prefer the curated `sorare_*` tools — e.g. for your own cards use `sorare_list_my_cards`, do NOT hand-write a `currentUser { cards { … } }` query. If you must write raw GraphQL, confirm field names with `sorare_graphql_schema(type:"…")` first. Common mistakes seen in the wild:
- On a card (`AnyCardInterface` / `Card`): the rarity field is **`rarityTyped`** (enum), NOT `rarity`.
- There is **no `footballPlayer`** field on a card — use **`anyPlayer { … }`** (or `player { … }` for the football-specific player).
- Field/type errors come back as `Field '…' doesn't exist on type '…'` — that means the name is wrong; introspect with `sorare_graphql_schema`, don't keep guessing.

loginBody.query: mutation SignInMutation($input: signInInput!) { signIn(input: $input) { currentUser { slug } jwtToken(aud: "${aud}") { token expiredAt } errors { message } } }

tools.name: sorare_current_user
tools.description: Get the authenticated Sorare user's profile (slug, email, nickname). Use this first to confirm the connection is healthy.

endpointMapping.path: query CurrentUser { currentUser { slug email nickname } }

tools.name: sorare_get_card_by_slug
tools.description: Fetch a single Sorare football card by slug (e.g. 'lionel-messi-2023-rare-1'). Returns player, rarity, season, current owner, and the live single-sale offer if any. For richer data use sorare_graphql_query with the relevant slice from sorare_graphql_schema.

slug.description: The card's unique slug from a card URL.

endpointMapping.path: query CardBySlug($slug: String!) { football { card(slug: $slug) { slug name rarity displayRarity backPictureUrl power season { startYear } user { slug } footballPlayer { slug displayName position country { slug } } liveSingleSaleOffer { id endDate status receiverSide { amounts { wei eurCents usdCents gbpCents } } } latestEnglishAuction { id endDate currentPrice currency } } } }

tools.name: sorare_search_player
tools.description: Search football players by name (full-text). Returns up to `pageSize` matches with slug, display name, country, and current club. Use the returned slug with sorare_list_player_cards or sorare_get_card_by_slug.

endpointMapping.path: query PlayerSearch($name: String!, $pageSize: Int!) { searchPlayers(query: $name, pageSize: $pageSize) { nbHits commonPlayerHits { anyPlayer { slug sport ... on Player { displayName country { slug } activeClub { name } } } } } }

tools.name: sorare_list_player_cards
tools.description: List cards minted for a given football player slug, with their rarity, season, and current owner.

endpointMapping.path: query PlayerCards($playerSlug: String!, $limit: Int!) { football { allCards(playerSlugs: [$playerSlug], first: $limit) { nodes { slug name rarity season { startYear } user { slug } } } } }

tools.name: sorare_list_my_cards
tools.description: List football cards owned by the authenticated user with rarity, player, and season. Pass `rarities` to filter (e.g. ['rare']).

rarities.description: Optional list of rarity tiers to keep.

endpointMapping.path: query MyCards($limit: Int!, $rarities: [Rarity!]) { currentUser { cards(first: $limit, rarities: $rarities, sport: FOOTBALL) { nodes { slug name sport ... on Card { rarity season { startYear } footballPlayer { slug displayName } } } } } }

tools.name: sorare_get_lineup
tools.description: Fetch a Sorare So5 lineup by id. Returns lineup metadata (name, draft/confirmable flags) and owning user. For appearance scores and bonuses use sorare_graphql_query with a fragment on So5Appearance.

endpointMapping.path: query Lineup($lineupId: ID!) { so5 { so5Lineup(id: $lineupId) { id name draft confirmable updatable bonuses { title key bonusBasisPoints } user { slug nickname } } } }

tools.name: sorare_wallet_balance
tools.description: Get the authenticated user's wallet balances in ETH (wei) and the three fiat reference currencies (EUR, USD, GBP). Useful before recommending a purchase.

endpointMapping.path: query MyBalances { currentUser { slug availableBalances { wei { wei } eurCents { eurCents } usdCents { usdCents } gbpCents { gbpCents } } } }

tools.name: sorare_user_by_slug
tools.description: Fetch another Sorare user's public profile by slug — nickname, status, and So5 lineup count. Use sorare_graphql_query with currentUser/cards for richer data.

slug.description: The user's slug (from the URL, e.g. 'harrypottah').

endpointMapping.path: query UserBySlug($slug: String!) { user(slug: $slug) { slug nickname status active suspended profile { verified badge { slug description } } } }

tools.name: sorare_player_recent_scores
tools.description: Return the most recent So5 scores for a football player slug, with position and game date. Use this to assess current form before composing a lineup or evaluating a card purchase.

last.description: Number of most-recent scores to return (1–20).

endpointMapping.path: query PlayerRecentScores($playerSlug: String!, $last: Int!) { players(slugs: [$playerSlug]) { slug displayName ... on Player { so5Scores(last: $last) { id position game { id date } } } } }

tools.name: sorare_token_prices
tools.description: Recent sale history for cards of a specific player + rarity. Returns an array of completed sales with date and amounts in wei / EUR / USD / GBP. Use to gauge fair market value.

season.description: Optional: filter by season start year (e.g. 2024 for the 2024-25 season).

endpointMapping.path: query TokenPrices($playerSlug: String!, $rarity: Rarity!, $first: Int!, $season: Int) { tokens { tokenPrices(playerSlug: $playerSlug, rarity: $rarity, first: $first, season: $season) { id date amounts { wei eurCents usdCents gbpCents } card { slug name } } } }

tools.name: sorare_get_auction
tools.description: Get a specific Sorare auction by id, including cards on auction, current price, best bid, and end date.

endpointMapping.path: query Auction($auctionId: String!) { tokens { auction(id: $auctionId) { id startDate endDate open cancelled currentPrice minNextBid currency anyCards { slug name ... on Card { rarity footballPlayer { slug displayName } } } bestBid { amounts { wei eurCents } userBidder { slug nickname } } bidsCount } } }

tools.name: sorare_my_trophies_summary
tools.description: Summary of the authenticated user's So5 results: total final rankings, podium finishes, and lifetime card / monetary rewards. Optionally filter by sport.

endpointMapping.path: query MyTrophies($sport: Sport) { currentUser { slug nickname so5TrophiesSummary(sport: $sport) { finalRankings podiumRankings cardRewards totalMonetaryRewards(referenceCurrency: EUR) { eurCents } } } }

tools.name: sorare_player_floor_price
tools.description: Get the cheapest currently-listed card for a player at a given rarity. Returns the card slug + the live single-sale offer price in wei + EUR/USD/GBP cents. Optionally restrict to in-season cards only. Far cheaper (1 round-trip) than scanning live_sale_offers when you just want 'what does this player's rarity X cost right now?'.

inSeasonOnly.description: If true, only consider cards eligible for in-season tournaments.

endpointMapping.path: query PlayerFloor($playerSlug: String!, $rarity: Rarity!, $inSeasonOnly: Boolean) { players(slugs: [$playerSlug]) { slug displayName ... on Player { lowestPriceAnyCard(rarity: $rarity, inSeason: $inSeasonOnly) { slug name ... on Card { rarity season { startYear } liveSingleSaleOffer { id endDate status receiverSide { amounts { wei eurCents usdCents gbpCents } } } } } } } }

tools.name: sorare_live_sale_offers
tools.description: List currently-live single-sale offers on the secondary market (Sorare's 'transfer market'). Optionally filter by player slug or sport. For rarity / max-price filters use sorare_graphql_query.

playerSlug.description: Optional: only offers for this player slug.

endpointMapping.path: query LiveSaleOffers($playerSlug: String, $sport: Sport, $limit: Int!) { tokens { liveSingleSaleOffers(playerSlug: $playerSlug, sport: $sport, first: $limit) { nodes { id endDate status senderSide { anyCards { slug name ... on Card { rarity season { startYear } footballPlayer { slug displayName } } } } receiverSide { amounts { wei eurCents usdCents gbpCents } } } } } }
